notes: Use this as an internal golden case first.
Do not reuse the raw contact card or raw screenshots in shared demos.
Any cross-tenant or public demo version should swap names and remove contact details.

space_notes: Client may bring in long tables so everyone can eat together
day_plan: Pool for most of the day
Return to the house for dinner
client_preference_notes: She is open to the chef's creativity

tone_notes: Warm and appreciative on both sides
Long response delays occurred, but the conversation recovered positively
Becca explicitly gave the chef creative control

timeline.text: Hey! I'm glad Anthony could get us connected. Let me know when you're free, we can chat about the 13th.
Awesome! Thank you so much! Is it okay if I reach back out Thursday and we can start figuring some stuff out?
Absolutely no rush at all. Thursday works for me!
And i'll be sure to talk to my sister before then and get an idea of the type of food she is looking for ishh haha

Hi David! I'm so sorry! Today got away from me catching up on things I needed to do! Any chance we can connect next week about dinner? Im off this sunday Sunday, and I'm off Monday Thursday and Friday if that works!
Hey sorry! I didn't see this. I'll be around, just let me know when. There's not rush

No worries! So what info can I provide to help you? Haha- it is gonna be 18 girls! My sister does like Mexican inspired food things- but she isn't super picky. but she is totally open to your creativity as well! We were figuring it'll be that Saturday night, June 13th- we were planning on doing like a chill day- prob coffee downtown in Kennebunkport and then grabbing sandwiches and hanging at the pool for most of the day- then get ready for the dinner at the house. I can send you some pics of the kitchen/ living room area- it's super open. We might bring some long tables in to eat all together and decorate those! Maybe thinking some hors d'oeuvres and - main course/ dessert? What are your thoughts?
Hey!!! This is a cute place. I can't believe I didn't see this message. I'm very sorry. This sounds perfect I can start making you guys a menu.

No worries! I didn't want to bombard you if you were busy at all! Yeah that would be great! I'm leaving the creativity in your hands! I can give you a call the end of next week some time if that gives you time to work something up?
Hey- it's Becca! Just checking in from last time we talked! Seeing where you are at with the possible menu for the Saturday bachelorette dinner! Thanks so much!
Hey! I'm so sorry for my awful response time. I'll be finishing up you options tonight, so we can discuss them on the phone. Youll have lots to pick from.
Sounds good! I unfortunately am away on vacation with friends this week-But I'll be free Sunday! Is there anytime that would be good to call?
Sent menu proposal titled Mexican-Inspired Dinner | June 13
Hopefully that message is good enough to just shoot out to everybody and up to your expectations. Enjoy your vacation.

This looks phenomenal-the message is above and beyond haha. I will look at it with my sister when I'm back and I look forward talking to you Sunday about what choices we want! Thank you again so much!!

notes: Primary planner for her sister's bachelorette dinner

event.special_requests: Mexican-inspired direction; enough variety for a group decision

space: Potential long tables for shared dining

menu_proposal.title: Mexican-Inspired Dinner | June 13
hors_doeuvres: Melon gazpacho with pickled peppers, cucumber, basil, cilantro, and cumin seed vinaigrette
Marinated cucumber salad with tomatillo cilantro vinaigrette
Grilled vegetable salad with lime vinaigrette, sweet corn, zucchini, red onion, peppers, and green beans
Guacamole, pico de gallo, and chips
Street corn with grilled corn, cotija, pimenton, and lime
Warm queso with fresh pickled vegetables, crema, and chips
Fried plantains with chipotle crema, lime, and cilantro
Scallop ceviche tostadas with orange, cilantro, and chili on a fried tostada
Crispy potato cheese wraps with jicama slaw and chipotle crema
Braised beef nachos with braised beef, cheddar, queso, salsa, sour cream, lime, and pickled onions
Marinated grilled skewers (choice of chicken, beef, or shrimp)
entrees: Fish tacos with bronzed haddock, pineapple salsa, chipotle mayo, avocado, and cotija
Birria tacos with braised beef, consomme, crispy cheese taco shell, and cilantro
Mini pork chalupas with a fried dough shell, smoked pork shoulder, cotija, and chipotle lime crema
Braised chicken thighs with mole enchiladas and Spanish rice
Marinated grilled flank steak with pupusas and cabbage slaw
desserts: Flan or chocoflan with blueberry gelato
Fried churros with chocolate sauce and strawberry gelato
Sweet corn gelato with candied corn and roasted strawberries
Strawberry tres leches with candied lime balm gelato
Chocolate and blueberry tart with salted caramel gelato
menu_proposal.menu_note: Menus are typically built around one primary entree. If the client includes two, they can be served split-style or divided across the group.

open_questions: Final selected hors d'oeuvres are unknown.
Final selected entree or entrees are unknown.
Exact address and access instructions are unknown.
Allergies and dietary restrictions beyond broad preference are unknown.
Pricing and deposit terms are not shown in this thread.
Whether the Sunday 1 PM call happened is unknown.

why_this_case_matters: Real client thread with partial information, delays, emotional tone, and a strong menu handoff.
Captures the transition from referral to brief to menu proposal to pending selection.
Good test case for both customer-facing communication and event-readiness systems.

applicable_systems.value: Tests extraction from informal, fragmented SMS instead of clean inquiry forms.
Tests turning conversational notes into a usable client record.
Tests tone recovery after response delays and maintaining trust.
Tests converting a loose brief into a structured choice menu.
Tests group-circulation messaging and pending-selection states.
Tests whether an event can be rehearsed with incomplete logistics and missing operational proof.

recommended_next_steps: Create a redacted clone of this case for reusable cross-tenant testing.
Seed this case into a dummy tenant as a real client, event, and menu.
Run the event through the Service Simulation panel, not just the generic Simulation Lab.
Capture the blockers Chef Flow surfaces and convert them into product requirements.
